notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: Connection exposes CreateConnection, GetConnection, ListConnections, UpdateConnection, and DeleteConnection, and the wider family also exposes ChangeConnectionCompartment plus ConnectionDiagnostics. Create, update, and delete responses each expose opc-work-request-id headers and GetWorkRequest returns OperationStatus values ACCEPTED, IN_PROGRESS, WAITING, FAILED, SUCCEEDED, CANCELING, and CANCELED.

Pinned OCI SDK contract: CreateConnectionDetails and UpdateConnectionDetails are polymorphic over connectionType MYSQL and ORACLE. The MySQL create shape requires technologyType, securityProtocol, databaseName, and the shared credential fields; the Oracle create shape requires technologyType plus the shared credential fields and optional connectionString or databaseId inputs. Update bodies expose the type-specific mutable fields but omit connectionType and technologyType.

Pinned provider facts: the provider surface for oci_database_migration_connection also includes ChangeConnectionCompartment and uses work-request polling for create, update, delete, and compartment-change flows. The reviewed controller-backed runtime does not publish ChangeConnectionCompartment or ConnectionDiagnostics and instead exposes only the base CRUD mutation surface.

Repo-authored bind-versus-create stays explicit: the reviewed runtime only attempts pre-create reuse when displayName, connectionType, and technologyType are all present, then narrows list candidates in memory with connectionType, technologyType, and observable type-specific identity fields such as databaseId, connectionString, databaseName, dbSystemId, host, and port.

Repo-authored mutation policy stays explicit: only the UpdateConnectionDetails surface reconciles in place. compartmentId, connectionType, and technologyType remain replacement-only drift for the published runtime even though provider facts include ChangeConnectionCompartment.

Repo-authored status projection and secret behavior stay explicit: the runtime projects the published status read-model fields from OCI responses, but intentionally excludes password, replicationPassword, and sshKey material from status. The row has no Kubernetes secret side effects.
